title: AACEClassification
description: Output of Agent: design-classifier. Reads an uploaded design package (PDF / IFC / DXF extraction summaries + a small set of page renders) and decides which AACE estimate class (per 18R-97 / 56R-08) the design supports — Class 5 / 4 / 3 / 2. Class 1 is never produced by an agent (full bid docs required). Extends pm_artifact_base via allOf so the artifact slots into the same Documents/Approvals plumbing as every other PM artifact.

project_label.description: Free-text label for this project / upload (e.g. 'QPB1 — DH-2 80% DD'). Provided by the user at upload time and echoed here for downstream routing/display.

class.description: AACE class the agent recommends. Class 1 and Class 0 are not allowed — full bid docs are required for Class 1, which is out of scope.

design_maturity_estimate_pct.description: Agent's best estimate of design completeness as a percentage. AACE bands: Class 5 = 0-2%, Class 4 = 1-15%, Class 3 = 10-40%, Class 2 = 30-70%.

accuracy_range.description: AACE-recommended accuracy band for the chosen class. The agent reports it explicitly so reviewers can sanity-check.

low_pct.description: Negative side of the accuracy band as a percentage (e.g. -50 for Class 5).

high_pct.description: Positive side of the accuracy band as a percentage (e.g. +100 for Class 5).

supporting_evidence.description: Why the agent chose this class. Each item names a category (scope completeness, structural detail, MEP detail, civil detail, schedule/duration, etc.), assigns a 0-1 score for that dimension, and cites the drawing evidence.

missing_for_next_class.description: What design info would be needed to support a higher (more accurate) class. Each item names a deliverable, why it matters, and which class it would unlock.

uploaded_files.description: Manifest of the files the user uploaded for classification. Status flags failed/partial extractions so reviewers can decide whether to re-upload.

design_disciplines_detected.description: Free-form tags for the disciplines the agent could see in the upload (e.g. architectural, structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, civil, fire-protection, security, BIM, controls). Useful downstream for the estimator-scheduler.

category.description: Free-form category label set by the agent (e.g., 'scope_completeness', 'design_completeness', 'system_clarity', 'scope_definition', 'mechanical_detail', 'electrical_detail', 'site_civil_detail', 'structural_detail', etc.). Conventional values are documented in the design-classifier prompt for routing consistency, but free-form tags are accepted so schema validation catches real bugs rather than prompt-style nuance.

score.description: 0 = nothing in the package supports this dimension; 1 = fully detailed.

evidence.description: Plain-English supporting note. Drawing references, sheet numbers, IFC entity counts, etc.

deliverable.description: What's missing (e.g. 'Structural framing plans for DH-2', 'Equipment schedule with model numbers').

rationale.description: Why it matters for the estimate.

would_unlock_class.description: Which AACE class would become supportable once this is delivered. Class 1 is excluded.

extraction_summary.description: Brief plain-English summary of what was extracted (page counts, entity counts, dominant content).

minio_key.description: Storage key for the file in the upload bucket. Optional; not required for the agent's output but useful for the runtime.
